message:
security(mcp): wire 6 direct authorize deny paths to policy_denied_audit (ft-mw1zb)

Closes 11 of 12 deny paths from the ft-hg6io matrix. Adds an async
helper `persist_mcp_policy_denial_async` (sibling of the sync
`persist_mcp_policy_denial` that the ft-rsqap gate uses) that
`runtime.block_on` handlers can call inline via the existing
`StorageHandle::record_policy_denial_audit(record).await` path — no
blocking bridge needed since these handlers already live in an async
context.

Wired sites (both the Deny and RequireApproval branches on each):

  wa.get_text        (mcp_tools.rs:~1296, Option<StorageHandle>)
  wa.search          (:~1706)
  wa.workflow_run    (:~2515, Option<StorageHandle>)
  wa.reserve         (:~3385)
  wa.release         (:~3536)
  wa.accounts_refresh(:~3795)

Each call emits both the existing `tracing::warn!` signal and a row in
policy_denied_audit with the stable const from
PolicyDeniedAuditRecord::{DECISION_*, REASON_CODE_*}. Best-effort:
`persist_mcp_policy_denial_async` swallows write errors into
tracing::warn! and returns, so audit-table failures never block the
client-facing policy-denied response.

One site deliberately NOT wired: wa.send. The `engine.authorize(&input)`
at :2348 is dry_run-only; the live-send path at :2364 routes through
`PolicyGatedInjector`, which handles policy decisions internally and
already writes denied attempts to the audit_actions table. Adding a
second write to policy_denied_audit from the wa.send handler would
double-count. Filed no follow-up; if fleet operators want a single
unified deny stream from PolicyGatedInjector, that's its own
architectural bead.

docs/security/read-path-redaction-matrix.md: note added to Actionable
Findings section recording 11-of-12 coverage and the wa.send rationale.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -61,6 +61,7 @@ Column meanings:
 ## Actionable findings from this audit
 
 - No open findings remain in the audited rows above as of `ft-yj375`; `wa.state` and `ft robot state` now redact pane `title` / `cwd` at the serving handler.
+- Policy-denial audit wiring (ft-h90rh / ft-rsqap / ft-mw1zb): 11 of 12 deny paths now also persist to `policy_denied_audit`. Only `wa.send` is deliberately routed through `PolicyGatedInjector` (which already writes to `audit_actions`); adding a second audit stream would double-count. See `docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md`.
 
 ## Regression discipline
